text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers.

We use Huffman encoding to compress

So here if we talk, let's suppose what are the characters A, B, C, D, E
If we talk about A, then what is the ASCII value of A? 97.
So if I want to represent 97, how many bits will we need?

What is 2 raised to power 7? 128. 
What is 2 raised to power 6? 64.

So this value is more than 64, so we can't
 represent in 6 bits, it will take 7 bits.
So if I want to represent A, I need 7 bits, B, C, D,

so here in total how many characteristics
 are there in the message? 30.
So total how many characters are there? 30, so 30 x 7 is what? 210.

Means I will need 210 bits to encode
 this message in a normal way.

But Huffman is a greedy approach, we will reduce 
the number of bits based on their frequency.

So here first of all if we talk about frequency in 
this type of question, you have to simply count,
most of the time frequencies are already given in the question
that A is repeated so many times, B, C, D.

Minimum element we have is A,
 means whose frequency is the minimum.

which is the minimum of these? 8.

so this is how we got 30 here

And then now you have to find what?
 You have to find the length.

To reach 7 means to reach B, so here

then how many nodes from the root, how many lines
